feedback.text: As an indie game developer and software engineer, I strongly advocate for the inclusion of video game preservation in the Digital Fairness Act. Consumers routinely lose access to purchased games when online services are shut down, an issue highlighted by the Stop Killing Games initiative. This undermines trust and constitutes misleading marketing. The Act should require clear disclosure of end-of-life policies and ensure basic access post-discontinuation, such as offline modes or sanctioned private servers. Digital fairness must include the right to retain access to purchased digital goods, particularly when games are marketed without a clear expiration date.
